Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: 1. Understand the Goal: The user wants me to analyze an event log against a normative process description, identify violating cases, and explain the violations based on the provided text.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2. Analyze the Normative Process Description: Start: Always begins with Receive order. Step 2: Check inventory must occur before payment authorization or fulfillment decisions. Branch 1 (Sufficient Inventory): Sequence: Receive -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship order -> Close order. Constraints: Authorize payment after Check inventory and before packaging. Package items after authorization and before shipping. Ship order after packaging. Close order is final. Branch 2 (Insufficient Inventory): Sequence: Receive -> Check inventory -> Cancel order -> Close order. Constraints: After cancellation, no fulfillment activities (packaging, shipping). Close order is final. Explicit Violations Mentioned: Shipping after cancellation. Authorizing payment before checking inventory. Packaging items before authorization. Any deviation from the stated sequences. Implicit/Key Rules: Receive order is first. Check inventory is second. Path splits after Check inventory: either Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship order -> Close order OR Cancel order -> Close order. Close order is always last. No skipping steps in the respective paths.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. Analyze the Event Log (Case by Case): Case 1: Sequence: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Package items -> Ship order -> Close order. Matches Branch 1 exactly. Verdict: Compliant. Case 2: Sequence: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Package items -> Authorize payment -> Ship order -> Close order. Violation: Packaging before payment authorization. Swapped order of Package and Authorize. Verdict: Violates normative process. Case 3: Sequence: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Cancel order -> Ship order -> Close order. Violation: Shipping after cancellation. Also, mixing fulfillment (Authorize payment) with cancellation path. Verdict: Violates normative process. Case 4: Sequence: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Cancel order -> Close order. Matches Branch 2 exactly. Verdict: Compliant. Case 5: Sequence: Receive order -> Check inventory -> Authorize payment -> Ship order -> Close order. Violation: Skipped Package items. Shipping directly after payment authorization without packaging. Verdict: Violates normative process.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. Synthesize Findings: Compliant: Cases 1, 4 Violating: Cases 2, 3, 5 Need to explain each violation clearly, referencing the text. Case 2 Violation: Swapped Package items and Authorize payment. Text states authorization must happen before packaging. Case 3 Violation: Ship order occurs after Cancel order. Text explicitly states shipping should never happen after cancellation, and the cancellation path should go directly to Close order. Case 5 Violation: Skipped Package items. Text states packaging must occur after authorization and before shipping.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 5. Draft Response (Mental Refinement): Structure the response clearly: State which cases violate the process. For each violating case, list the sequence, identify the specific violation, and quote/reference the normative description. Briefly mention compliant cases for completeness. Ensure tone is analytical and directly addresses the prompt. Check against prompt: Identify which cases... Explain the violations... referencing the textual description... interpreting any ambiguities. All steps covered.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Verification during drafting: Case 2: Sequence is Receive -> Check -> Package -> Authorize -> Ship -> Close. Violation: Package before Authorize. Text: Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging... Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping. Clear. Case 3: ... Violation: Ship after Cancel... Case 5: Missing Package... Cases 1 & 4: Match the two defined paths exactly. Compliant. Everything aligns perfectly with the prompt.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Note during thought: Wait, lets double check Case 3. The text says: if the inventory check reveals that there is not enough stock... proceed to Cancel order. Once canceled... next reasonable step is Close order. Authorizing payment before canceling might be questionable, but the explicit rule violation is shipping after cancellation. Ill stick to the most explicit violations but note the path inconsistency if relevant. The prompt says: Any deviation... such as shipping after cancellation... Case 5: Missing Package items. The text says the sequence is Receive, Check, Authorize Payment, Package, Ship, Close. Skipping a step is a deviation. All good.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Verification during output gen prep: I will make sure to quote the relevant parts of the normative description for each case... All solid. Final check of the prompt... The paths are pretty clear, no major ambiguities. Ill just stick to the explicit rules. Ready.
